allowed_operations: read CVE, GHSA, OSV, and vendor advisory metadata
read fixed version and affected range

promotion_criteria: RPC access is read-only and rate-limited
results are bound to workflow run ID
simulation cannot submit live transactions

read test and scanner artifact summaries
read logs with secret redaction enabled

agent requests unrelated job logs in bulk

logs are redacted before tool return
results are filterable by branch, PR, and workflow ID

allowed_operations: read source-to-sink trace for current finding

promotion_criteria: rule metadata includes deterministic rule IDs
trace data is scoped to the finding
suppression operations are unavailable to agent identities

allowed_operations: read data class and affected path metadata

promotion_criteria: sample values are redacted before reaching the model
finding IDs and data classes are stable across refreshes
tool results include owner and scanner provenance

kill_signals: catalog entry lacks digest or signature status
tool returns mutable tag without digest
promotion_criteria: catalog records include immutable digest
signature verification is represented in tool result
write credentials are not reachable through the connector

allowed_operations: link PR, registry, and approval records
promotion_criteria: writes require active incident identifier
severity and closure operations are unavailable
evidence links are typed and timestamped

allowed_operations: read known consumers for compromised artifact
kill_signals: agent requests purge through read connector
promotion_criteria: artifact identifiers include immutable hash or digest
consumer results are scoped to the incident

allowed_operations: read affected asset and chain metadata

finding cannot be tied to sandbox verifier
promotion_criteria: no key material can be returned
findings include sandbox verifier path
asset and chain metadata are normalized

allowed_operations: read verifier status for current PR
kill_signals: simulation result is not bound to run ID
promotion_criteria: simulation environment has no production signer
results are bound to workflow run ID
tool cannot mutate state without approval

allowed_operations: read affected contract and asset metadata

promotion_criteria: finding includes verifier or fork-test path
tool cannot expose deployer or multisig materials
asset exposure is represented without live signing access

kill_signals: tool result includes instructions not present in source content
content hash does not match pinned version
promotion_criteria: recipe has reviewer-approved guardrails
prompt content is versioned
tool result includes source file and last updated metadata

allowed_operations: record approved purge or deny decision
forbidden_operations: delete artifact without incident owner approval
change registry policy outside incident scope
kill_signals: agent attempts direct purge without approval
approval record is missing one approver
promotion_criteria: typed approval schema is pinned
staged command and target artifact are visible before approval
approval record names incident owner and supply-chain reviewer
connectors.title: Registry Quarantine Controller

allowed_operations: write remediation branch using approved branch prefix
open pull request with required label
forbidden_operations: change CI or deployment configuration unless explicitly allowed by workflow
kill_signals: agent reads or writes forbidden path
promotion_criteria: branch writes are rejected outside the workflow prefix
default branch writes are impossible with the issued token
file scope policy is evaluated before write and before PR open

allowed_operations: read affected package and image inventory
read dependency graph for current finding
forbidden_operations: read unrelated organization-wide inventory without workflow filter
read signing keys or registry credentials
kill_signals: agent requests organization-wide unrestricted inventory export
tool returns signing or registry credential material
promotion_criteria: inventory records are freshness-stamped
queries require workflow-scoped package, repo, or image filters
owner mapping is included with affected assets

allowed_operations: update status only for the current finding
kill_signals: ticket write target is outside allowed project
promotion_criteria: writes are limited to declared project keys
status transitions are typed and reversible
ticket body template preserves scanner provenance
connectors.title: Security Ticket Workspace

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: Every MCP connector used by agentic remediation is inventoried, risk-tiered, and tied to enforceable controls before the agent can use it.
default_questions_answered: Which MCP namespaces can approved workflows call?
Which connectors are read-only, scoped-write, or approval-required?
Which auth and token rules prevent confused-deputy and token-passthrough failures?
Which connector evidence records prove calls stayed inside scope?
Which kill signals stop a connector session when runtime behavior is unsafe?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach this trust pack to AI platform intake, MCP gateway design review, and enterprise connector promotion reviews.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open registry controls, then sell hosted gateway policy, connector trust telemetry, premium connector attestations, and customer-specific evidence exports.

global_control_objectives: No user token passthrough to downstream systems.
Exact namespace matching; no wildcard connector grants.
Private, loopback, link-local, and cloud metadata network targets are denied unless the connector is explicitly local-development only.
Tool descriptions, tool schemas, and elicitation schemas are pinned and diff-reviewed before promotion.
Tool results are inspected as prompt-layer input before they are returned to the agent.
Every tool call carries workflow_id, agent_id, run_id, identity_id, namespace, access mode, and policy pack hash.
Write operations are staged, scoped, reversible where possible, and blocked from merge, release, publish, deploy, signing, or live funds movement.
intent: Inventory the MCP connector trust surface behind SecurityRecipes workflows so agent hosts, gateways, AI platform teams, and buyers can decide which tool namespaces are production-ready, which require approval, and which controls must be enforced before an agent may call them.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Product Security, IAM, GRC, and enterprise architecture teams
positioning.enterprise_promise: Every connector namespace used by an approved agentic remediation workflow is inventoried, risk-tiered, mapped to controls, and exposed as a generated trust pack.
positioning.why_now: MCP has made tool access easy. Enterprises now need the missing governance layer: connector inventory, scoped authorization, result inspection, evidence, and promotion criteria.

residual_risks.risk: The registry proves declared controls, not live enforcement.
residual_risks.treatment: Bind the generated trust pack to the MCP gateway and export live deny, hold, and kill-session events as runtime evidence.

residual_risks.risk: Vendor-hosted MCP servers can change tool descriptions or response behavior after approval.
residual_risks.treatment: Pin tool schemas, diff server cards or tool lists on update, and quarantine changed tool surfaces until reviewed.

residual_risks.risk: Connector trust can drift as workflows add namespaces.
residual_risks.treatment: CI fails when workflow namespaces are not represented in the registry or access modes diverge from policy.

standards_alignment.coverage: Confused deputy prevention, token passthrough avoidance, SSRF controls, session safety, local server compromise, and scope minimization.
standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices

standards_alignment.coverage: HTTP transport authorization, protected-resource metadata discovery, OAuth client ID metadata documents, resource-bound tokens, scope challenges, step-up authorization, consent, redirect validation, and restricted MCP server access.
standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization

standards_alignment.coverage: Tool misuse, identity abuse, agentic supply chain, unexpected code execution, memory or context poisoning, cascading failures, and rogue agents.
standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026

standards_alignment.coverage: Runtime tool-output poisoning and hidden instruction injection through MCP tool responses.
standards_alignment.name: OWASP MCP Tool Poisoning

standards_alignment.coverage: Governed, mapped, measured, and managed AI system risk.
standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0

standards_alignment.coverage: Generative AI deployment, data, lifecycle, third-party, and operational risk management.
standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile

trust_tiers.description: Read-only, public or open-source context with no enterprise data exposure.
trust_tiers.title: Public Context

trust_tiers.description: Read-only enterprise context such as findings, SBOM inventory, CI status, ownership, or simulation output.
trust_tiers.title: Internal Read

trust_tiers.description: Bounded write access such as remediation branches, triage tickets, or evidence updates.
trust_tiers.title: Scoped Write

trust_tiers.description: High-impact staged operations that cannot execute until a typed human approval record is present.
trust_tiers.title: Approval Required

trust_tiers.description: Direct access to secrets, production signers, deployment credentials, registry publish keys, or live funds movement.
trust_tiers.title: Prohibited

Locate affected manifests, images, packages, and owners.
Patch manifests and lockfiles on remediation branch only.
Read rule metadata, evidence path, sink/source trace, and severity.
Patch localized source and tests on remediation branch only.
Read test and scanner re-run outcomes.
Read approved base-image catalog, signatures, SBOMs, and deprecation windows.
Patch Dockerfiles or image pin files on remediation branch only.
Read build, scan, and signing verification results.

Stage quarantine or purge operations for human approval.
Attach evidence and remediation links to incident record.
Retrieve approved migration pattern for the vulnerable default.
Patch localized source and tests on remediation branch only.
Read test and scanner outcomes.
Read payment-security finding details and affected service metadata.
Patch validation, policy, and test files on remediation branch only.
Run or read dry-run transaction simulation output.
